headline: Shake Shack stumbles as fast‑food demand softens; shares plunge
summary: STORY: Shares of Shake Shack plunged as much as 30% on Thursday and were heading for their worst day ever after the restaurant chain reported a quarterly profit loss and missed Wall Street's revenue estimates.Shake Shack said it was hurt by rising commodity costs, including beef, and weak consumer spending.And it's not the only fast‑food chain seeing consumers tighten their belts.McDonald's, Domino's and Papa John's all reported weaker quarterly sales growth, signaling pressure on consumer spending from rising gas prices driven by the U.S. war in Iran.Companies like Chipotle and Restaurant Brands International have also flagged rising beef prices, which have set records due to dwindling U.S. cattle supplies.Shake Shack executives said on a post-earnings call that the company's short-term results will continue to be impacted by the ongoing war in the Middle East.

raw.headline: Shake Shack stumbles as fast‑food demand softens; shares plunge
raw.summary: STORY: Shares of Shake Shack plunged as much as 30% on Thursday and were heading for their worst day ever after the restaurant chain reported a quarterly profit loss and missed Wall Street's revenue estimates.Shake Shack said it was hurt by rising commodity costs, including beef, and weak consumer spending.And it's not the only fast‑food chain seeing consumers tighten their belts.McDonald's, Domino's and Papa John's all reported weaker quarterly sales growth, signaling pressure on consumer spending from rising gas prices driven by the U.S. war in Iran.Companies like Chipotle and Restaurant Brands International have also flagged rising beef prices, which have set records due to dwindling U.S. cattle supplies.Shake Shack executives said on a post-earnings call that the company's short-term results will continue to be impacted by the ongoing war in the Middle East.

headline: McDonald's misses US sales growth target as consumer spending tightens
summary: STORY: McDonald's missed its U.S. sales growth target in the first quarter.The world's biggest burger chain posted U.S. same‑store sales growth of 3.9% - below Wall Street estimates.Thursday's update showed low-price meal deals and limited-time offers struggled to draw diners.Consumers have recently pulled back on spending due to higher fuel and grocery costs.CEO Chris Kempczinski said the operating environment was still "challenging."That was depsite McDonald's beating quarterly estimates for revenue and profit.Several U.S. restaurant chains like Wingstop and Domino's have reported weaker quarterly sales growth.They cited a hit to customer spending from soaring gasoline prices caused by the Iran war.Wall Street analysts also argue lower-income consumers have turned more selective.It's seen buyers trade down to simpler, single‑item orders rather than full meals.Data from Placer.ai showed U.S. traffic was uneven at McDonald's through the first quarter.Same-store visits fell 1.3% in January due to winter storms.Traffic bounced back just under 4% in February on pent‑up demand, but slipped again in March.On a global scale, McDonald's found comparable sales rose 3.8% - slightly behind analyst forecasts.Although it was an improvement on last year's numbers.The firm's total revenue of $6.52 billion beat estimates.

headline: McDonald's CEO says consumer spending could be 'getting a little bit worse'
summary: Shares of McDonald's have fallen 10% over the last year, hurt by concerns about the broader economy.
